description: A botched Kubernetes namespace migration has broken the connectivity between 5 microservices in the 'ecommerce-platform' namespace. The services were previously operational but 5 separate failures were introduced during the migration. An architecture reference document at /home/ga/Desktop/service_architecture.md describes the intended service topology and expected connectivity. Your job as the SRE on-call is to restore full connectivity by diagnosing and fixing all 5 failures without being told which specific resources are broken. Login to Rancher at https://localhost (cluster: 'local') with credentials admin / Admin12345678!.

injected_failures: api-gateway Service selector typo: 'app: api-gw' instead of 'app: api-gateway' (no endpoints)
product-service env var INVENTORY_HOST points to wrong namespace FQDN
NetworkPolicy 'restrict-cart-egress' blocks port 3000 egress from cart-service to payment-service
payment-service ConfigMap 'payment-config' has NOTIFICATION_HOST with wrong namespace
inventory-db Service port is 5433 instead of 5432

verification_criteria.C2: product-service deployment env INVENTORY_HOST contains 'ecommerce-platform' FQDN
verification_criteria.C3: NetworkPolicy allows port 3000 egress from cart-service (no blocking rule on 3000)
verification_criteria.C4: payment-config ConfigMap NOTIFICATION_HOST contains correct namespace 'ecommerce-platform'
